annotations.label: Combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Combinatorics is an area of mathematics primarily concerned with counting

annotations.note: Mathlib has a `Mathlib/Combinatorics/` directory but no top-level definition of the field itself.

annotations.label: Scope of combinatorics

anchor.snippet: combinatorics is involved with: the enumeration (counting) of specified structures

annotations.note: This is a meta-description of the field, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Rhind papyrus problem 79

anchor.snippet: The earliest recorded use of combinatorial techniques comes from problem 79 of the Rhind papyrus

annotations.note: Historical anecdote; not a formal statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sushruta's 63 combinations of tastes

anchor.snippet: Indian physician Sushruta asserts in Sushruta Samhita that 63 combinations can be made out of 6 different tastes

annotations.note: The count 2^6 − 1 = 63 nonempty subsets is a special case of `Finset.card_powerset`, but the specific story is not formalized.

annotations.label: Symmetry of binomial coefficients

anchor.snippet: established the symmetry of binomial coefficients

annotations.note: `Nat.choose_symm` states `choose n k = choose n (n-k)`, the symmetry of binomial coefficients.

annotations.label: Enumerative combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Enumerative combinatorics is the most classical area of combinatorics and concentrates on counting the number of certain combinatorial objects

annotations.note: Subfield description; Mathlib has `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Enumerative/` but no formal definition of the area.

annotations.label: Twelvefold way

anchor.snippet: The twelvefold way provides a unified framework for counting permutations

annotations.note: `Mathlib/Data/Sym/Card.lean` cites the Twelvefold Way and formalizes one of its 12 cells, but the full framework is not unified.

annotations.label: Analytic combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Analytic combinatorics concerns the enumeration of combinatorial structures using tools from complex analysis

annotations.note: Subfield description; no Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Partition theory

anchor.snippet: Partition theory studies various enumeration and asymptotic problems related to integer partitions

annotations.note: Integer partitions are defined in `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Enumerative/Partition/`, but the field itself is not a formal object.

annotations.label: Graph theory considerations

anchor.snippet: Graphs are fundamental objects in combinatorics

annotations.note: Graphs are formalized as `SimpleGraph`, but the meta-claim about their fundamental role is not a theorem.

annotations.label: Design theory

anchor.snippet: Design theory is a study of combinatorial designs

annotations.note: No combinatorial-design definitions found in Mathlib by grep for `Design`/`BlockDesign`/`SteinerSystem`.

annotations.label: Kirkman's schoolgirl problem

anchor.snippet: such as in Kirkman's schoolgirl problem proposed in 1850

annotations.note: No occurrences of `Kirkman` found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finite geometry

anchor.snippet: Finite geometry is the study of geometric systems having only a finite number of points

annotations.note: `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Configuration.lean` covers projective-plane-style finite incidence configurations, but the field is not defined as such.

annotations.label: Order theory

anchor.snippet: Order theory is the study of partially ordered sets

annotations.note: Partial orders are extensively formalized under `Mathlib/Order/`, though the subfield itself is not a formal object.

annotations.label: Matroid theory

anchor.snippet: Matroid theory abstracts part of geometry

annotations.note: Matroids are defined in `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Matroid/Basic.lean` with substantial accompanying API.

annotations.label: Extremal combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Extremal combinatorics studies how large or how small a collection of finite objects

annotations.note: Subfield description; `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Extremal/` exists but the field has no formal definition.

annotations.label: Sperner's theorem

anchor.snippet: The latter question is answered by Sperner's theorem

annotations.note: `IsAntichain.sperner` bounds antichains in `Finset α` by the middle binomial coefficient — Sperner's theorem.

annotations.label: Largest triangle-free graph

anchor.snippet: the largest triangle-free graph on 2n vertices is a complete bipartite graph

annotations.note: Turán's theorem is formalized and specialises (r=2) to Mantel's theorem for triangle-free graphs.

annotations.label: Ramsey theory

anchor.snippet: Ramsey theory is another part of extremal combinatorics. It states that any sufficiently large configuration will contain some sort of order

annotations.note: The Hales–Jewett theorem (a Ramsey-type result) is formalized, but Ramsey's theorem itself is not stated directly.

annotations.label: Probabilistic combinatorics

anchor.snippet: In probabilistic combinatorics, the questions are of the following type

annotations.note: Subfield description; no formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Probabilistic method

anchor.snippet: This approach (often referred to as the probabilistic method ) proved highly effective

annotations.note: Grep finds no occurrences of `probabilistic method` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Algebraic combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Algebraic combinatorics is an area of mathematics that employs methods of abstract algebra

annotations.note: Subfield description; no formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Combinatorics on words

anchor.snippet: Combinatorics on words deals with formal languages

annotations.note: No Mathlib module dedicated to combinatorics on words was found.

annotations.label: Geometric combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Geometric combinatorics is related to convex and discrete geometry

annotations.note: Subfield description; no formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cauchy's rigidity theorem

anchor.snippet: the Cauchy theorem on the rigidity of convex polytopes

annotations.note: Grep for `Cauchy.*rigidity` and `rigidity.*polytope` returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Topological combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Combinatorial analogs of concepts and methods in topology are used to study graph coloring

annotations.note: Subfield description; no formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arithmetic combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Arithmetic combinatorics arose out of the interplay between number theory

annotations.note: `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Additive/` contains substantive additive/arithmetic-combinatorial results (Plünnecke–Ruzsa, Freiman, Roth, etc.), but the subfield itself is not defined.

annotations.label: Additive number theory

anchor.snippet: Additive number theory (sometimes also called additive combinatorics) refers to the special case when only the operations of addition and subtraction are involved

annotations.note: Many additive number theory results are in `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Additive/`, but the subfield itself has no formal definition.

annotations.label: Infinitary combinatorics

anchor.snippet: Infinitary combinatorics, or combinatorial set theory, is an extension of ideas in combinatorics to infinite sets

annotations.note: Subfield description; no formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Combinatorial optimization

anchor.snippet: Combinatorial optimization is the study of optimization on discrete and combinatorial objects

annotations.note: `Mathlib/Combinatorics/Optimization/ValuedCSP.lean` covers valued constraint satisfaction, a slice of combinatorial optimization, but the field is not defined.

annotations.label: Coding theory

anchor.snippet: Coding theory started as a part of design theory with early combinatorial constructions of error-correcting codes

annotations.note: No dedicated coding-theory module found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discrete geometry

anchor.snippet: Discrete geometry (also called combinatorial geometry) also began as a part of combinatorics

annotations.note: Subfield description; no formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Combinatorial dynamical systems

anchor.snippet: Combinatorial aspects of dynamical systems is another emerging field

annotations.note: Subfield description; no formal definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ising model exact solution

anchor.snippet: Examples include an exact solution of the Ising model

annotations.note: Grep for `Ising` returns no hits in Mathlib.
